channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Trump’s birthright citizenship ban may fail — but the administration already got too far
item.description: On Wednesday morning, the Supreme Court heard arguments in Trump v. Barbara, a case challenging President Donald Trump's 2025 executive order banning birthright citizenship. Justices seemed skeptical of the administration's argument, but by taking up birthright citizenship at all, they showed how much ground nativists have gained since Trump's first term. The 14th Amendment is […]...

item.title: Cash App launches ‘buy now, pay later’ feature for P2P pay transfers
item.description: Block says that the new feature has strong built-in protections to keep users from so-called debt spirals.

item.title: NASA’s Artemis 2 Mission Blasts Off
item.description: NASA's Space Launch System rocket, with the Orion spacecraft atop, launched at 6:35 p.m. ET on Wednesday, kicking off the first mission to send astronauts to the Moon in over 50 years.

item.title: Disney’s Robot Olaf Dying Is the Funniest Thing to Happen in 2026
item.description: The AI-powered 'Frozen' animatronic freezing up and taking a little tumble is far more entertaining as a meme than if the magic snowman worked as designed.

item.title: Quordle hints and answers for Thursday, April 2 (game #1529)
item.description: Looking for Quordle clues? We can help. Plus get the answers to Quordle today and past solutions.

item.title: NYT Connections hints and answers for Thursday, April 2 (game #1026)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.

item.title: NYT Strands hints and answers for Thursday, April 2 (game #760)
item.description: Looking for NYT Strands answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, including the spangram.

item.title: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie receives a worse rating than its predecessor, as critics call it ‘a bland screensaver that’s worse than AI’
item.description: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie has performed poorly among critics, with one reviewer comparing it to AI-generated content.

item.title: De-fi platform Drift suspends deposits and withdrawals after millions in crypto stolen in hack
item.description: Blockchain trackers put the cryptocurrency heist in the hundreds of millions of dollars and is already on track to be the largest crypto theft in 2026 so far.

item.title: NASA launches four astronauts toward the Moon on the Artemis II mission
item.description: NASA's Artemis II flight, which is set to take four astronauts toward the Moon for the first time in more than 50 years, successfully launched on Wednesday evening. The Artemis II mission, part of NASA's Artemis program that's intended to bring humans back onto the Moon as early as 2028, will bring the four astronauts […]

item.title: Kia’s EV3 Is Headed to the US This Year With Up to 320 Miles of Range
item.description: There's set to be a new, compact EV in town soon.

item.title: 'Defying logic': This quantum battery actually charges faster the bigger it gets
item.description: This quantum device absorbs energy efficiently, accelerates charging with scale, and stores power temporarily, though it's still limited in practical output.

item.title: Anthropic took down thousands of GitHub repos trying to yank its leaked source code — a move the company says was an accident
item.description: Anthropic executives said it was an accident and retracted the bulk of the takedown notices.

item.title: This Is the Way to Lego’s April Releases
item.description: Next month might be the traditional big 'Star Wars' drop from Lego, but April is all about 'The Mandalorian and Grogu'.

item.title: This iOS 18 patch could protect your device from a vicious malware strain — here’s why you need it now
item.description: Apple has patched iOS 18 to protect users from the DarkSword malware, even if they have an older device.

item.title: Play nice with PDFs for life with this $30 app
item.description: Work smarter, not harder with PDFs thanks to AcePDF Converter and Editor lifetime license, on sale now for $29.99 (reg. $99.99).

item.title: Group Pushing Age Verification Requirements for AI Turns Out to Be Sneakily Backed by OpenAI
item.description: It gave the leader of a nonprofit involved with it "a very grimy feeling."
